name: customers-backend-schemas
description: Create Pydantic schemas for customers module: CustomerSummary, CustomerDetail, CustomerExclusion, CustomerListResponse.
instruction:
Create ITRP/backend/app/schemas/customers.py following the pattern in schemas/autobuy.py.

Schemas needed:
1. CustomerSummary: customer_id (str), customer_name (str), open_amount (Decimal), currency (str), invoice_count (int), country (str)
2. CustomerDetail: extends CustomerSummary with: legal_entity_code (str), credit_limit (Decimal|None), current_exposure (Decimal), is_excluded (bool), exclusion_reason (str|None), sales_type_override (str|None)
3. CustomerExclusionRequest: customer_id (str), reason (str)
4. CustomerExclusionResponse: customer_id (str), customer_name (str), excluded_by (str), excluded_at (datetime), reason (str)
5. CustomerListResponse: items (list[CustomerSummary]), total (int), tab (str: 'active'|'all'|'previously_purchased')

Follow existing patterns: use Field() for validation, Decimal for amounts.
acceptance_criteria: schemas/customers.py exists with all 5 schema classes
Schemas use Pydantic BaseModel with proper types
Decimal used for monetary amounts

produces.schemas: CustomerSummary, CustomerDetail, CustomerExclusionRequest, CustomerExclusionResponse, CustomerListResponse in schemas/customers.py

name: customers-firestore-collection
description: Add CUSTOMER_EXCLUSIONS collection to collections.py. Create customers service with Firestore operations for exclusions.
instruction:
1. Add to ITRP/backend/app/collections.py:
   CUSTOMER_EXCLUSIONS = 'customer_exclusions'

2. Create ITRP/backend/app/modules/customers/__init__.py (empty)

3. Create ITRP/backend/app/modules/customers/service.py with functions:
   - async def get_customer_exclusions(db, country: str) -> dict[str, dict]:
     Read all docs from CUSTOMER_EXCLUSIONS where country == country. Return {customer_id: {reason, excluded_by, excluded_at}}
   - async def exclude_customer(db, customer_id: str, country: str, reason: str, user_email: str) -> dict:
     Write to CUSTOMER_EXCLUSIONS collection. Doc ID: f'{country}_{customer_id}'. Include audit trail entry.
   - async def include_customer(db, customer_id: str, country: str, user_email: str) -> None:
     Delete from CUSTOMER_EXCLUSIONS. Include audit trail entry.

Follow patterns from modules/schedule/repository.py for Firestore operations.
Follow patterns from modules/audit/service.py or audit/router.py for audit trail recording.
Use app.firestore.get_db() for database client.
acceptance_criteria: CUSTOMER_EXCLUSIONS constant in collections.py
customers/service.py has get_customer_exclusions, exclude_customer, include_customer
All mutations write audit trail entries

produces.collection: customer_exclusions in Firestore with docs: {customer_id, country, reason, excluded_by, excluded_at}

name: customers-backend-router
description: Create customers router with endpoints: list customers (from BQ), get customer detail, exclude/include customer. Register in main.py.
instruction:
1. Create ITRP/backend/app/modules/customers/router.py with:

   router = APIRouter(prefix='/api/customers', tags=['customers'])

   GET /api/customers?country={code}&tab={active|all}&search={text}&page={n}&page_size={n}&sort_by={field}&sort_order={asc|desc}
   - tab=active: customers with open_amount > 0 in current week's invoices
   - tab=all: all distinct customers from temp_invoice
   - Query BigQuery temp_invoice: SELECT DISTINCT customer_id, customer_invoice_name, customer_billing_country_code, SUM(open_invoice_amount) as open_amount, COUNT(*) as invoice_count FROM temp_invoice WHERE customer_billing_country_code = @country GROUP BY customer_id, customer_invoice_name, customer_billing_country_code
   - For 'active': add WHERE open_invoice_amount > 0
   - Merge with exclusions from Firestore (is_excluded flag)
   - Return CustomerListResponse
   - Requires permission: purchase:read or reporting:read

   GET /api/customers/{customer_id}?country={code}
   - Detail view: invoices for this customer from BQ + credit limit info + exclusion status
   - Return CustomerDetail

   POST /api/customers/{customer_id}/exclude
   - Body: CustomerExclusionRequest
   - Requires permission: purchase:execute
   - Calls exclude_customer() from service
   - Return CustomerExclusionResponse

   DELETE /api/customers/{customer_id}/exclude?country={code}
   - Requires permission: purchase:execute
   - Calls include_customer() from service
   - Return 204

   GET /api/customers/excluded?country={code}
   - List all excluded customers for a country
   - Reads from Firestore CUSTOMER_EXCLUSIONS

2. Register in main.py:
   from app.modules.customers.router import router as customers_router
   app.include_router(customers_router)

Follow patterns from modules/buying/router.py for BigQuery queries.
Follow patterns from modules/rbac/router.py for permission checks (require_permission).
Use from app.auth.dependencies import get_current_user, require_permission.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/customers returns customer list from BigQuery with exclusion status
GET /api/customers/{id} returns customer detail with invoices
POST /api/customers/{id}/exclude creates exclusion in Firestore with audit trail
DELETE /api/customers/{id}/exclude removes exclusion with audit trail
Router registered in main.py
All endpoints check RBAC permissions

name: customers-frontend-page
description: Create My Customers page with 3 sub-tabs (Active, All, Previously Purchased), customer table with search/sort, and exclude action.
instruction:
Create ITRP/frontend/src/app/customers/page.tsx

Follow patterns from admin/page.tsx (tab structure, table, modals).
Use Ant Design components: Tabs, Table, Input.Search, Button, Tag, Modal.
Use TanStack Query for data fetching (pattern from dashboard/page.tsx).

Page structure:
1. Three Tabs: 'Active customers ({count})' | 'All customers ({count})' | 'Previously Purchased'
2. Search bar above table
3. Table columns:
   - Customer/Group ID (blue link, clickable → opens detail modal)
   - Customer name (link)
   - Open amount (formatted with currency)
   - Actions: Eye icon (detail preview), Exclude button (if has permission)
4. Detail modal (Ant Design Drawer or Modal):
   - Customer info header
   - Invoice list for this customer
   - Credit limit info (if available)
   - Exclude/Include button

API calls:
- GET /api/backend/api/customers?country={selected}&tab={tab}&search={text}
- POST /api/backend/api/customers/{id}/exclude (body: {customer_id, reason})
- DELETE /api/backend/api/customers/{id}/exclude?country={code}

Permission checks:
- Use PermissionGate from components/auth/PermissionGate.tsx
- Exclude button: only show if hasPermission('purchase', 'execute')
- Page access: require hasPermission('purchase', 'read') or hasPermission('reporting', 'read')

Country selector:
- Reuse the country fetching pattern from itrp/page.tsx (GET /api/buying/countries)
- Default to first country or user's scoped country

Update navigation in components/layout/AppLayout.tsx:
- 'My Customers' nav item should link to /customers (it already exists in nav but goes nowhere)
acceptance_criteria: /customers page renders with 3 tabs
Customer table shows data from backend API
Search filters table by customer name or ID
Eye icon opens detail view with customer invoices
Exclude button creates exclusion (with reason input)
Include button removes exclusion
Permission gates hide exclude button for read-only roles
Navigation links to /customers correctly
exclusions: DO NOT modify other pages except AppLayout nav link

name: customers-integration-test
description: Verify full customer flow end-to-end: list → detail → exclude → verify exclusion shows in list → include → verify removed.
instruction:
Manual verification checklist:
1. Start containers: docker compose -f docker-compose.local.yml up
2. Login as admin@randstad.com
3. Navigate to /customers
4. Verify Active customers tab shows customers from BQ
5. Verify All customers tab shows more customers
6. Search for a customer name — verify filter works
7. Click eye icon — verify detail modal opens with invoice list
8. Click Exclude on a customer — enter reason — verify exclusion saved
9. Refresh page — verify excluded customer shows is_excluded tag
10. Click Include to remove exclusion — verify tag removed
11. Login as opco.pl@randstad.com — verify only PL customers visible
12. Login as opco.pl — verify Exclude button not visible (no purchase:execute permission)

If any step fails: create a bug task in Forge pipeline.
acceptance_criteria: All 12 verification steps pass
